inspection_labels.name_or_mission: CAPSTONE (Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment)
inspection_labels.function: Lunar NRHO orbit validation and autonomous navigation technology demonstration for NASA Artemis/Gateway program
key_components: rectangular main bus (~22x22x34 cm, microwave-oven size)
two deployable rectangular solar panel wings (dark blue/black solar cells with silver grid lines)
gold/silver MLI thermal blanket wrapping the bus (Kapton foil with visible wrinkles)
S-band and X-band communication antennas (flat patch or small protruding modules)
star trackers and sun sensors (small circular apertures on bus surface)
micro-propulsion thruster nozzles at one end (small conical metal nozzles)
inspection_labels.visual_appearance: A small gold/silver rectangular box with two dark blue rectangular solar panel wings deployed on opposite sides

survey_viewpoints.description: left-side observation viewpoint with the service spacecraft orbiting into the target-left sector while keeping the target near the camera center

service_sites.description: one valid service-ready site; there may be multiple such sites
service_sites.notes: Any site listed here can satisfy pre-service-alignment.

inspection_labels.function: Map the interaction between solar wind and interstellar medium by detecting energetic neutral atoms (ENAs) at the heliospheric boundary
key_components: octagonal main bus (~58 cm high x 95 cm across, bus-tire size)
surface-mounted solar array on top deck (always Sun-pointing)
two ENA imager sensors: IBEX-Hi (gold cylindrical) and IBEX-Lo, perpendicular to solar panels
long communication antenna protruding from top
cone-shaped thrusters for spin-axis maintenance
micro-meteoroid shield over propulsion tanks (silver bump on structure)
two small square metal heat radiators on front panel
inspection_labels.visual_appearance: A small octagonal satellite (~bus-tire size) with a flat solar-panel-covered top deck, two prominent cylindrical ENA sensor apertures on the side, and a long antenna from the top

inspection_labels.name_or_mission: BioSentinel (deep space radiation biology experiment, launched aboard Artemis I)
inspection_labels.function: Study effects of deep space radiation on living organisms (yeast) beyond Earth's magnetosphere to support future long-duration human exploration
key_components: 6U CubeSat bus (~10x20x30 cm, shoebox-sized, 14 kg)
two deployable solar panel wings (30 W total)
X-band transponder and antenna for Deep Space Network communication
microfluidics biology payload cards with yeast cells
Linear Energy Transfer (LET) spectrometer for radiation dosimetry
onboard data storage and processing electronics
inspection_labels.visual_appearance: A very small elongated rectangular CubeSat (shoebox-sized) with two thin deployable solar panel wings on opposite sides

inspection_labels.name_or_mission: New Horizons (first Pluto flyby and Kuiper Belt exploration)
inspection_labels.function: First reconnaissance of the Pluto system, flyby of Kuiper Belt object Arrokoth, and ongoing exploration of the outer solar system
key_components: triangular/wedge-shaped aluminum main bus body with honeycomb panels
large 2.1-meter high-gain dish antenna (dominant front-facing feature, white/light-colored)
RTG (Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generator) on a cylindrical boom extending from the rear
LORRI long-range reconnaissance imager telescope
Ralph visible/IR imager and Alice UV spectrometer
SWAP and PEPSSI particle/plasma instruments
no deployable solar panels (entirely RTG-powered)
inspection_labels.visual_appearance: A compact triangular/wedge-shaped body dominated by a large white 2.1m high-gain dish antenna on front, with a dark cylindrical RTG boom extending from the rear; no solar panels

inspection_labels.name_or_mission: Huygens (part of Cassini-Huygens mission, first probe to land on Titan)
inspection_labels.function: Atmospheric descent and surface landing on Saturn's moon Titan; measured atmospheric composition, pressure, temperature, wind profiles, and surface conditions
inspection_labels.status: nominal (mission complete, landed on Titan January 14 2005)
key_components: disc/saucer-shaped aeroshell with 2.75m diameter conical front heat shield
back cover shell protecting the descent module
descent module housing six scientific instruments
three-stage parachute system (pilot chute, 8.3m main parachute, 3.03m stabilizer drogue)
DISR descent imager/spectral radiometer camera
HASI atmospheric structure instrument, GCMS gas chromatograph mass spectrometer, SSP surface science package
gold/copper-colored MLI thermal insulation on exterior surfaces
inspection_labels.visual_appearance: A saucer/disc-shaped probe (~2.7m diameter) with a conical front heat shield and a domed back cover, gold-colored thermal insulation visible on the exterior
